observations.recommendation:
Nevertheless, we recommended and Management agreed that the GFPS and GFP:
Coordinate closely with the Municipal Budget Officer (MBO) and other concerned Department Heads in monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure correctness of the reported GAD accomplishments and fund utilization; and
Submit a revised or supplemental GPB to the concerned DILG office in case additional GAD PPAs were identified and/or implemented, or supplemental funds were appropriated.
We also recommended and Management agreed that the Department Heads monitor the implementation of their targeted PPAs to facilitate the timely reprogramming and reallocation of the allotted funds, if needed, to other prioritized PPAs to maximize the utilization thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Update the composition of the existing inventory, appraisal and disposal committee to ensure that the personnel assigned can perform their duties and responsibilities as members of the said committees;
Conduct the semestral physical count of inventory of supplies and materials; and
Submit the RPCI as prescribed in the NGAS Manual for LGU.

We recommended and the Management agreed that the MA and the MT conduct regular reconciliation of the MGD’s cash accounts to decrease, if not to eliminate, the noted discrepancies.  Also, present in the Notes to FSs the amount of discrepancy between the MAO and the MTO by year as a manner of monitoring the difference in records of the subject office.

We recommended and management agreed that:
All regular purchases shall be coursed through the inventory account, except those purchased out of the petty cash fund, which shall be for immediate use;
The GSO or the property supply/officer shall consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit the SSMI together with the original copy of the RIS to the MA; and
The MA shall prepare the JEV based on the SSMI submitted by the GSO or property/supply officer to record in the appropriate expenditure accounts.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE require the MDRRMO to:
Follow up on the requested resolution from the Office of the SB and strictly monitor the implementation of the targeted PPAs and the utilization of the LDRRMF; and
Coordinate closely with the MBO and MA to determine the unutilized balance of LDRRMF to facilitate timely reprogramming and reallocation of the fund for its optimum utilization.

We recommended and Management agreed, that the LCE direct:
The MA to prepare adjusting entry to correct the TL-LDRRMF in the TF books and to eliminate the discrepancies noted in the CIB balances and unutilized amounts of LDRRMF; and
The MDRRMO, MA and MBO to conduct periodic reconciliation of their records to ensure the correctness of the reported amounts for the appropriation and utilization of the LDRRMF.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Continue to issue demand letters compelling the AOs or employees to settle immediately in full their outstanding CAs, either by liquidation, by payment/refund or through salary deductions;
Ensure that all CA are liquidated at year-end in compliance with COA Circular No. 97-002 (d); and
Comply strictly with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002, and all related rules and regulations pertaining to granting and liquidation of CAs.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
Require the concerned implementing office/s of the MGD to step up their concerted efforts to ensure optimum utilization and implementation of the PPAs funded by NGAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desired benefits to its constituents;
Return the excess funds of previously completed PPAs to their respective SAs as prescribed under COA Circular No. 94-013; and
Continue to retain copies of MOA/guidelines as a basis for the utilization of the subject fund transfers and furnish the AT for proper evaluation and timely verification of the implementation and disbursements made in relation to these trust accounts.

We commended Management for continuously extending assistance to the national government through faithful compliance with duties as a withholding and remitting agent of taxes in accordance with tax laws, rules and regulations.
Likewise, we recommended and the Management agreed to continue to remit all withheld taxes within the prescribed period as set by the BIR.

We commended Management for their compliance to GSIS rules and regulations on withholding and remittance of premium contributions and loan repayments.
Further, we recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of GSIS contributions in accordance with Section 6 (b) of RA No. 8291.

We recommended and Management agreed that the LCE should continuously comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on deductions and remittance of HDMF contributions in accordance with the provisions of RA No. 9679.

We recommended and Management agreed to continuously comply with existing laws, rules and regulations on proper deduction and remittance of PhilHealth contributions in accordance with Section 18 of the Revised IRR of RA No. 7875 as amended by RA Nos. 9241 and 10606.

observations.recommendation:
We commended Management for the proper monitoring of its cash position thereby ensuring that all of the MGD’s obligations and funds reserves are backed up by actual cash.  

Nevertheless, we recommended that Management:

Continue to exercise diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of the MGD’s funds while limiting expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid cash deficiency;

Invest the idle cash in major infrastructure projects or other programs, projects and activities (PPA) that can help improve the livelihood and general welfare of the MGD’s constituents. However, relative to the audit observation no. 4 on the discrepancy of cash balances between the records of the MAO and the Municipal Treasury Office (MTO), the said Offices shall reconcile first their records to arrive at the correct amount of cash savings that will be available for appropriation; and

Require the finance committee to prepare a Cash Analysis monthly/quarterly and submit the same to the Local Chief Executive (LCE) for reference in decision-making purposes.

observations.recommendation:

Return the excess funds of previously completed PPAs to their respective SAs as prescribed under COA Circular No. 94-013; and

Continue to retain copies of Memorandum of Agreement (MOA)/guidelines as basis for the utilization of the subject fund transfers and furnish the AT for proper evaluation and timely verification of the implementation and disbursements made in relation to these trust accounts.

We commended the MGD, through the continuous efforts and dedication of its GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) and GAD Focal Person (GFP), for (a) maintaining the GAD Database; (b) enactment of the LGU’s GAD Code; (c) observing the timelines in the preparation and submission of the GAD Plan and Budget (GPB) and GAD Accomplishment Report (AR); and (d) expediting the implementation of the GAD PPAs thus, substantially utilizing the allotted GAD funds for CY 2023.

Nevertheless, we recommended that the GFPS and GFP:

Coordinate closely with the MBO and other concerned Department Heads in monitoring the implementation of GAD PPAs to ensure the correctness of the reported GAD accomplishments and fund utilization;

Submit a revised or supplemental GPB to the concerned DILG office in case additional GAD PPAs were identified and/or implemented, or supplemental funds were appropriated; and

Submit copies of the approved GPB and GAD AR to the AT within the period prescribed in COA Circular No. 2014-001.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the LCE:
Consider the creation of a new Inventory Committee that will perform diligently annual comprehensive physical inventory count of all MGD’s properties;
Require the MAO and PSO to maintain complete and updated PPELCs and PCs, respectively, conduct periodic reconciliation of PPE records and adopt the applicable procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020 for the one-time cleansing of MGD’s PPE accounts; and
Require the MAO to effect necessary adjustments.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to undertake the procedures necessary for the relocation/re-identification and proper disposal of all listed articles/properties in the IIRUP as at December 31, 2018 as well as those identified to be useless this period.
We likewise recommended that after disposal, the Municipal Accountant (MA) drop from the books the accounts and balances of the disposed items of PPE.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendations that the LCE:
Direct the MA to hasten the preparation of the BRS for two TF accounts and immediately submit the complete set of BRS to the AT for proper validation and audit;
Instruct the MA and the Municipal Treasurer (MT) to reconcile periodically their respective books/records so that discrepancies in their balances do not accumulate and errors are immediately detected and corrected; and
Effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts as soon as reconciling items were identified and verified in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the FS as at year-end.
We recommended that the LCE direct MT to prepare the CRAAF on monthly basis based on the individual RAAFs of all AOs and submit the same to the AT not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the LCE direct the MAO to compel the concerned AOs to settle immediately in full or refund their outstanding CAs and comply strictly with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and all related rules and regulations with regard to the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.
We also recommended that the MAO assess the collectability of the outstanding CAs of the deceased AO and if warranted, submit a request for write-off in accordance with the provisions of COA Circular 2016-005.

We recommended that the LCE require the MAO to:
Exert more effort in tracing/reconciling the transactions and prepare adjusting entry/ies, as appropriate;
Make the necessary request for write-off to COA for those accounts determined uncollectible, following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
In case of favorable decision granting the authority to write-off, transfer the gross amount of the receivables to the Registry of Accounts Written-Off and maintain the same, including the records and documents pertaining thereto.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the Municipal Budget Officer and the MA to observe strictly the proper recognition of a liability.  Henceforth, obligations should be recognized only when the goods are already delivered or services are rendered pursuant to above-cited regulations.
We also recommended that payment for late/delayed deliveries of purchased supplies/goods be deducted of liquidated damages equivalent to one-tenth (1/10) of one percent for every day of delay.

We commended the efforts exerted by the MGD in ensuring prompt implementation of most of the listed PPAs funded by various NGAs.  However, we recommended that the LCE instruct the offices concerned to comply strictly with the implementation period as indicated in the Circular or MOA, if any. Also, hasten the planning and procurement process to facilitate full implementation of PPAs funded therefrom.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Development Council and all other implementing offices of the MGD step up its concerted efforts to ensure optimum utilization of the 20% DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes.

We recommended that Management require:
All concerned AOs to submit the accounts and financial reports for each month to the MAO on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the aforementioned provisions of laws, rules and regulations; and
The MAO to comply strictly with the submission of required monthly accounts and financial reports, together with all the supporting documents, on or before the legal deadlines in compliance with the above-cited regulations.

We recommended that the LCE require:
The Municipal Treasurer to provide the correct SET income estimate for the year based on the projected RPT collections;
The LSB and the MBO to coordinate properly so that all prior years’ unexpended balances that needs to be reprogrammed, net of continuing appropriations, are included in the SEF Budget computation; and
The LSB to prepare the SEF Budget in accordance with the timeline set forth in DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 01, s. 2017.

We recommended that the LSB ensure the efficient implementation and immediate realization of identified priority education PPAs in order to achieve the optimum utilization of SEF and achievement of its objectives.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the LCE:
Direct the Municipal DRRM Council to monitor properly the utilization of the LDRRMF by ensuring the efficient and effective implementation of PPAs to promote a more disaster resilient community and help reduce disaster risks;
Compel the OIC-MDRRM Officer to comply religiously with the submission of Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of the LDRRMF, certified correct by the MA, to the AT on or before the 15th day of the following month; and
Give priority to the creation of mandatory DRRM positions and hiring of qualified personnel to fill up the positions in compliance with JMC No. 2014-1 to address the lack of workforce of the MDRRMO.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management direct the heads of the Municipal Accounting Office (MAO), Property and Supply Office and the Inventory Committee to step up their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements (FS).

We also recommended that Management study the guidelines and adopt the procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management direct the heads of the Municipal Accounting Office, Property and Supply Office and the Inventory Committee to step up their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements (FSs).
We also recommended that Management study the guidelines and adopt the procedures prescribed by COA Circular No. 2020-006 dated January 31, 2020.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management require the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to undertake the procedures necessary for the relocation/re-identification and proper disposal of all listed articles/properties in the IIRUP as at December 31, 2018 as well as those identified to be useless this period.
We likewise recommended that after disposal, the Municipal Accountant drop from the books the accounts and balances of the disposed items of PPE.

We recommended that Management direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the Municipal Accountant a Local Road Network Inventory with data as to components, fair value, condition, and the life expectancies of Infrastructure Assets particularly the Road Networks.
We also recommended that Management direct the Municipal Accountant to:
Segregate and recognize the cost of each component of the local roads with identified cost and recognize component without corresponding cost at its fair value;
Prepare and maintain complete PPELC-LRNLC for each item of road network; and
Recompute the accumulated depreciation and depreciation expense and thereafter prepare the necessary adjusting entries to correct the year-end balances of the Public Infrastructure and Government Equity accounts.

We recommended that Management require the:
Inventory Committee to willfully conduct the required physical count of all inventory items, prepare the corresponding RPCI and submit the same to the Audit Team within the prescribed period;
Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for the acquisition of supplies and materials; and record the subsequent issuance to the related expense accounts on the basis of the SSMI to be prepared by the Property and Supply Officer; and
Municipal Treasurer or the acting Storekeeper, in his behalf, and the Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the SCs and the SLCs, respectively, and to conduct periodic reconciliation of the Inventory records in accordance with the existing rules and regulations.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of the BRS for SEF and two TF accounts and immediately submit the complete set of BRS to the Audit Team for proper validation and audit;
Instruct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to periodically reconcile their respective books/records so that discrepancies in their balances do not accumulate and errors are immediately detected and corrected; and
Effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts as soon as reconciling items were identified and verified in order to reflect the correct balance of the cash account in the FSs as at year-end.

We recommended that Management, through the Municipal Accounting Office, to:
Compel the concerned AOs to immediately settle in full or refund their outstanding cash advances, either by liquidation, by direct cash payment or through salary deductions. Require the AOs to provide a definite period to which deductions shall be made to fully pay their dues, the period of which must not exceed one year; and
Strictly comply with the requirements of Section 89 of PD No. 1445, COA Circular No. 97-002 and all related rules and regulations with regard to the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances.

We recommended that Management require the Accounting Office to exert more effort in tracing/reconciling the transactions, determine those deemed uncollectible and make the necessary request for write-off to COA, following the guidelines and procedures as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant trace and analyze the pertinent transactions of the Intra-Agency accounts Due from/Due to Other Funds, reconcile the difference/s and prepare adjusting entry/ies, as necessary.

We recommended that Management strictly comply with the requirements under COA Circular No. 2012-001 and ensure keeping copies of the MOAs for proper evaluation of the implementation and timely verification of the veracity and completeness of the disbursements made in relation to these trust accounts.

We recommended that Management:
a.	Require the Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records with the records of the SAs as to the unliquidated, unrefunded and/or unremitted balances; and
b.	Immediately remit, refund or return any remaining balances to the SA after the completion of intended PPAs.
We recommended that Management make the necessary adjusting entries to reclassify the said balances to their appropriate accounts in order to fairly present all affected accounts in the FS.

We recommended that Management make the necessary adjusting entries and proper reclassification of the said balances in order to fairly present all affected accounts in the FS. Further, we recommended that the Accounting Office exert more effort in tracing the sources and/or purpose of those balances that need further validation for proper recording and presentation in the FS.

We commended the Management for its faithful compliance with the provisions of LBC No. 125, particularly with the reportorial requirements as to the utilization and implementation of the grant, thus, enhancing transparency and proper accountability of the BGCM. However, we recommended the immediate creation of the LGU’s website in compliance with all other regulations requiring such posting.
Further, we recommended the full utilization of the grant through realignment/s of the fund to important PPAs in order to address the Municipality’s current COVID-19-related needs.

We reiterated our previous years’ recommendation that the Municipal Development Council (MDC) and all other implementing offices of the Municipality step up its concerted efforts to ensure optimum utilization of the 20 per cent DF and implement the PPAs within the targeted timeframe to achieve the timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental management outcomes.

We recommended that Management ensure that the 20 per cent DF be used solely for development programs and projects and that no amount is utilized for expenditure items not in accordance with the pertinent provisions of DILG-DBM JMC No. 2017-01.
We recommended that LSB adhere to the format presented in Form 1 of DepEd-DBM-DILG JC. No. 1, s. 2017, in the proper computation and presentation of the SEF Budget.

We recommended that the LSB strictly follow the prescribed timeline in the preparation of the annual SEF Budget as provided in the JC and ensure efficient implementation and maximum utilization of the fund.

We reiterated our prior years’ recommendation that Management use the unexpended and excess balances of the LDRRMF by incorporating them in the LDRRMFIP and submit the same, including Supplemental Investment Programs, if any, to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
We recommended that Management ensure the efficient implementation and optimum utilization of the LDRRMF in order to build a more disaster resilient community and to reduce disaster risks.

We recommended that Management give priority to the creation of mandatory DRRM positions and hiring of qualified personnel to fill up the positions in compliance with MDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1.

We commended Management for exerting efforts by implementing programs, activities and strategies on SWM and enforcement of the provisions of the Ecological SWM Act of 2000 for the protection of public health and preservation of the environment.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that Management ensure that the procedures in the post closure land use plan be strictly carried out. These include inspection, maintenance and monitoring after the implementation of closure activities which shall maintain the integrity and compliance of the closed dumpsite.

We recommended that Management direct the heads of the Municipal Accounting Office, Property and Supply Office and the Inventory Team to step up their commitment and efforts to comply with the conduct of annual physical inventory of all its properties and its reconciliation with the books and records of the Accounting and Property Office, to ensure a reliable balance of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We also recommended that Management direct the:
Appraisal and Disposal Committee to facilitate the immediate disposal of unserviceable properties in accordance with the most appropriate mode of disposition of property as provided in COA Circular No. 92-386 which are either through (a) public auction; (b) sale through negotiation; (c) transfer without cost to other offices or departments or other government agencies; or (d) by destruction; and
Municipal Accountant to make necessary entries to derecognize the unserviceable properties from the books of the LGU after the duly conducted disposal procedure.

We recommended that Management:
Require the Municipal Engineer to provide the Inventory Committee with complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects to facilitate the preparation of RPCLRN; and
Direct the Inventory Committee and the GSO to prepare and submit the RPCLRN and RLRN, respectively, in accordance with the formats prescribed in COA Circular No. 2015-008.
We also recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the GSO prepare and maintain separate subsidiary records/LRNLCs and LRNPCs, respectively, for local road projects for monitoring purpose and to ensure proper recording/presentation and control of the assets.

We recommended that Management require the:
Inventory Committee to step up its effort to conduct and finish semestral inventory count of all inventory items and to prepare the corresponding RPCI duly reconciled with the property and accounting records;
Municipal Treasurer or the acting Storekeeper, in his behalf, prepare and maintain SCs for each commodity/stock; and
Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain the corresponding SLCs and conduct periodic reconciliation of the records of the Property and Supply Office and Accounting Office in accordance with existing rules and regulation.
We also recommended that the LCE prioritize the provision of an area and/or room in the municipal building that will accommodate and ensure the safekeeping of supplies and materials under the custody of the Municipal Storekeeper.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to:
Strictly observe the perpetual inventory method in accounting for the acquisition and issuance of supplies and materials; and
Record the issuance of supplies and materials to the related expenses account on the basis of the SSMI.
We likewise recommended that Management require the Property and Supply Officer to consolidate weekly the RIS for which supplies and materials were issued using the SSMI and submit the same, together with the original copies of RIS, to the Office of the Municipal Accountant.

We recommended that Management instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to strictly observe the correct timeline for the release of the barangay share from RPT collection to each barangay pursuant to Section 271(d) of RA No. 7160.

We recommended that Management require:
The Local Finance Committee to:
Conduct a systematic and realistic forecasting of revenue, taking into account prior years’ experience and statistics as aids in setting targets;
Review the present budgeting to set a clearer guide for the concerned revenue generating departments towards attaining the targets set; and
Provide targets on accounts that did not have projections which should be based on statistics, prior years’ experience and future expectations.
The Municipal Treasurer’s Office (MTO) and other departments involved in generating revenues endeavor to perform efficiently in the revenue administration by considering, among others, the following:
Conduct a periodic review to ascertain whether collection targets are attained and in case of shortfall in collections, to analyze the reasons and to look into alternative actions to avoid incurrence of lapses in projection in the future; and
Intensify further the collection in the operation of Economic Enterprise and real property taxes and avail of administrative and judicial remedies in enforcing payments particularly in tax delinquency.

We recommended that the SB and concerned officials revisit the provisions of the Revenue Code of the Municipality to make possible amendments that would redound to the best interest of the LGU and the citizenry without going beyond the provisions and requirements of the LGC of 1991.

We recommended that Management:
Implement the projects and programs within the targeted timeframe to achieve timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development outcome of the Municipality; and
Instruct the Municipal Treasurer to employ strategies to maximize the lease of stalls in the new Public Market Building.

We recommended that Management:
Require the concerned AO to submit the accounts and financial reports to the Treasurer and thereafter to submit to the Accounting Office all the disbursement vouchers and payrolls, official receipts, including the corresponding reports of each month on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month or pursuant to the aforementioned pertinent provisions of laws, rules and regulations, as applicable; and
Direct the Accountant to strictly comply with the timelines on the recording and submission of accounts to the Auditor, within the first 10 days of the ensuing month, as required by the aforementioned existing law, rules and regulations and look into the possibility of allowing them to render overtime in order to submit the accounts and financial reports on the mandated deadlines.

We recommended that Management:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to hasten the preparation of BRS and submit them to the Audit Team, together with the corresponding JEVs/paid checks /original copies of Debit and Credit Memos for review within the reglementary period; and
Take appropriate immediate remedial measures, such as allowing them to render overtime services in order to update the BRS and meet the mandated deadlines.

We recommended that Management require the LSB to:
Maximize the implementation of identified programs to ensure efficient utilization of the fund so that its objectives are fully achieved; and
Strictly comply with the requirement of Section 272 of R.A. No. 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017 in identifying the PPAs that reflect and equitable distribution of mandated priority projects/programs.
We also reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendations that Management require the LSB to prepare the LSB three-year program supported by approved SIPs and other information from DepEd Regional Office, which supposedly were the basis for the preparation and approval by the LSB of the SEF Budget, and to furnish the Audit Team for reference and review purposes.  Henceforth, the LSB shall strictly comply and follow the mandated required processes and documents in planning and budgeting for SEF under the revised guidelines on the use of SEF pursuant to the above-mentioned JC.

We reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendation that Management step up its concerted efforts in the implementation of PPAs under the 20 per cent DF so that all PPAs are realized, financially settled within the targeted timeframes and that timely delivery of desirable socio-economic development and environmental outcomes is achieved.

We reiterated our previous years’ audit recommendation that Management re-appropriate all prior years unexpended and excess balances of the LDRRMF by incorporating such unexpended prior years’ balances in the LDRRMF Investment Plan for the specific period.
We also recommended that Management compel the LDRRM Officer to religiously comply with the preparation of Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF certified correct by the Municipal Accountant and prompt submission of the same to the concerned offices pursuant to the mandates of the relevant law and guidelines.

We recommended that Management require:

The Municipal Accountant to prepare and maintain complete and updated PPE Ledger Cards especially for Land, Construction in Progress and Infrastructure Asset accounts to support the GL accounts balance;

The Property Officer - Designate to prepare and maintain the Property Cards per type/category of properties owned by the Municipality; and

The Municipal Accountant and the General Services Officer/Municipal Treasurer to prioritize the reconciliation process and ensure that discrepancies noted in the reconciliation of PPE records be immediately investigated and adjusted in the books accordingly.

acceptance in compliance with Section 39.4 of the Revised
IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant require the
contractor to submit an irrevocable standby letter of credit of
equivalent value from a commercial bank, a bank guarantee
or a surety bond callable upon demand, issued by a surety or
insurance company duly licensed by the Insurance

Revised IRR of RA 9184, before allowing the 15% advance
payment on infrastructure contracts. Henceforth, he shall see
to it and ensure that contractors abide with the implementing
rules and regulations of RA 9184 in all procurement
activities of the local government unit.

contractor to provide/submit a Contractor’s All Risk
Insurance (CARI) upon award of the contract for
infrastructure projects. In the event that the contractor fails to
submit it, the municipality may acquire a CARI for the
project and make the contractor reimburse for the cost of its
premium. The procuring entity has also the right to impose
other sanctions against the Contractor that fails to provide
the required insurance as stated in Clause 15 of the General
Conditions of the Contract.

Require the drivers to indicate the odometer reading in the driver’s trip ticket, and if andc. odometer is out of order, require the supply officer to repair or replace it at once in order to determine the actual gasoline consumption; . Set guidelines on the limitation of gasoline consumption; and . Require the submission of Monthly Report of Official Travels and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumptions to the Office of the Auditor.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied with the audit recommendations. The drivers are required to indicate odometer reading and Monthly Reports of Fuel Consumptions are required to be submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. strictly adhere with the provisions of R.A. No. 9003; b. implement and maximize the use of Material Recovery Facility; c. create Solid Waste Management Board and prepare Solid Waste Management Plan of the Municipality; d. secure the agricultural lot for Demo Farm, Nursery, and Composing Site; and e. exert continuous information, education and communication campaign to the public on the importance of proper solid waste management.
observations.managementAction: Solid Waste Management Plan was already prepared and submitted to DENR.
observations.reason: No available Personnel with expertise on Solid Waste Management Implementation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. strictly comply with Section 272 of the Local Government Code in identifying programs/projects/activities chargeable against the SEF and require an equitable distribution of the Fund among the legally-mandated priority activities, in view of the fact that the DepEd has its own budget; and b. plan thoroughly the SEF budget to avoid non-implementation of projects/activities and monitor strictly to anticipate the timely re-allocation of funds for other allowable activities in lieu of those that cannot be implemented. c. see to it that all proposed projects are implemented without delay.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied with the auditor’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management: a. adopt specific guidelines on the use of post-paid plans stipulating who are entitled; b. limit mobile subscriptions and ensure adequate monitoring; c. require the Municipal Accountant to ensure adequate documentation of claims mandated under COA Circular No. 2012-001 before payment are effected; d. take advantage of promotions offered by telecommunication companies regarding unlimited calls or texts for as low as P699 per month.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied with the auditor’s recommendations. Will require elected officials to use the post-paid plans after election. Billing statements are required for the payment of the post-paid plans.
observations.reason: Minimum contract for the post-paid plans is two years except for the upcoming election, elected officials were not required to use the post-paid plans. Third quarter of 2016 elected officials will be required to use the post-paid plans.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with COA Circular No. 2012-001 and Section 362 of GAAM, Volume I that provides for the procedure, documentation and policy for spare parts requisition and repairs of motor vehicles. We likewise recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant to ensure completeness and propriety of supporting documents before processing payments of claims for repairs and maintenance of motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. strictly comply with DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-001 dated April23,2011; b. revert the 2009 and 2010 continuing appropriations to the general fund for appropriate budget re-enactment; c. require the Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant to reconcile their records and ensure proper charging of expenses under the 20% Local Development Fund; and d. ensure 100 per cent implementation of the 20% development fund as embodied in the Annual Investment Plan of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: Reprogrammed prior years. The management has exerted efforts to fully utilize and implement projects under the 20% EDF. Strict observance with the laws, rules and regulations in the procurement process.
observations.reason: Time constraints. Budget was approved only on February, 2014 and implemented in 2015. The municipality strictly follows the IRR of RA 9184 in the procurement/bidding process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended for the management to conduct physical inventory taking of all its inventories regularly and should be reported through the preparation of RPCI as prescribed by existing accounting regulations.
observations.managementAction: The requesting department are required to prepare issue slip upon purchase requisition and submission of the same to the accounting office for recording of expenses. Will lobby for the creation of the property and inventory custodian if budget permits.
observations.reason: Non-submission of the signed request slip; Budget limitation and prioritization.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the management to conduct physical inventory taking of all its assets regularly and should be reported through the preparation of RPCPPE as prescribed by existing regulations, We likewise recommended that the accounting office maintain the corresponding PPE ledger cards for each property in accordance with pertinent provisions of NGAS.
observations.managementAction: Created the Inventory Team to do the physical inventory of all its assets. Reconciliation of movable assets per accounting records and those of the property custodian were done in preparation for the inventory count.
observations.reason: Lack of time and manpower to do the actual physical count.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management strictly comply with AO No. 135 and DBM Budget Circular Nos. 2006-1 and 2012-4, thus, resulting in the irregular disbursement of funds and therefore, transactions were disallowable in audit.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied with the auditor’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. impose the collection of fees for the rental of cemetery lots; b. adhere strictly to the proper maintenance and supervision of the public cemetery; and c. consider replacing/expansion of cemetery.
observations.managementAction: The Management is looking for a bigger ideal lot to be used as public cemetery since there is no ordinance for the same.
observations.reason: No basis for the collection of Fees since there is no ordinance for the same.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. Require the Municipal Accountant to maintain a complete set of subsidiary ledgers for the above mentioned Special Accounts in the General Fund and to prepare separate financial statements thereof at year-end pursuant to Section 313 of RA 7160 and pertinent provisions of NGAS Manual, Vol. I for monitoring and control purposes. b. Instruct the Accounting Department to prepare monthly report of operations to be monitored and evaluated periodically by management.
observations.managementAction: The management prepares separate income statement due to tourism activities only. The public utilities and enterprises in the municipality were not declared as such by the Sangguniang Bayan.
observations.reason: Public utilities and enterprises in the municipality were not declared as such by the Sangguniang Bayan.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: a. Management initiate measures to collect the delinquencies amounting to P463,244.71 and employ strategies to intensify collection of delinquent market fees to generate additional income for development projects and to improve its collection efficiency. b. The Local Sangguniang Bayan enact a new tax ordinance to adjust the rates to current levels and impose stricter penalties or sanctions for non or late payments of market rentals, mayor’s permit and business taxes to ensure that businessmen will comply with their obligations and avoid incurrence of delinquencies.
observations.managementAction: The management has complied with the auditor’s recommendations.

observations.recommendation: We also recommended that management implement fully the projects under the 20% EDF projects.
observations.managementAction: The management has exerted efforts to fully utilize and implement projects under the 20% EDF. Strict observance with the laws, rules and regulations in the procurement process.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant exert the effort to reconcile the Due From and Due to LGUs with the Provincial Accountant to eliminate the_discrepancies.and effect the necessary adjustments to present accurate and reliable balances in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Will request for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Other Priorities

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management require the retired employees concerned or nearest kin to refund the overpayment and pay the understatement of terminal leave benefits to the family.
observations.managementAction: Management has complied with the auditor’s recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management: a. review these non-moving accounts, reconcile the same with other related accounts, if possible, and prepare the necessary adjustments, if any; and b. if the review shows that these accounts have been dormant or non-moving for more than five years or if review and analysis is not possible, the same should be requested for write off pursuant to COA Circular No. 97-001.
observations.managementAction: Will request for reconciliation.
observations.reason: Records are not available for reconciliation.
